channel.title: Trending Tech News - Krynn Tools
channel.description: Latest trending news in technology, AI, and cybersecurity. Curated from trusted sources, updated automatically.

item.title: Run large language models at home, BitTorrent‑style
item.description: Article URL: https://petals.dev/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=49015735 Points: 19 # Comments: 3
item.dc:creator: Hacker News

item.title: Codeberg Bans Cryptocurrency Projects
item.description: Article URL: https://codeberg.org/Codeberg/org/pulls/1254 Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=49015588 Points: 69 # Comments: 41
item.dc:creator: Hacker News

item.title: Lego’s Donkey Kong arcade machine lets Mario jump endless barrels — Miyamoto is reportedly happy
item.description: Carl Merriam has designed some of my favorite nostalgia-inducing Lego sets, including the Lego Nintendo Game Boy and Piranha Plant. He's assisted on the incredible Lion Knights' Castle, Galaxy Explorer, and Pirates of Barracuda Bay. Now, he's helped the company create a Donkey Kong arcade machine se
item.dc:creator: The Verge

item.title: After shocking quarter, IBM insists that AI isn’t killing the mainframe
item.description: After IBM's stock crashed last week on warnings of poor mainframe sales, the CEO explained that AI wrecked corporate hardware budget, temporarily.
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch

item.title: Anthropomorphism in Children's Interactions with LLM Chatbots
item.description: Article URL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.18250 Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=49014537 Points: 25 # Comments: 18
item.dc:creator: Hacker News

item.title: Meta won’t have to face the next planned social media addiction trial
item.description: Less than a week before Meta's lawyers were set to return to a Los Angeles courtroom, the plaintiff accusing the platform of inflicting harm dropped the case. Brought by 15-year-old Florida plaintiff going by initials R.K.C., the case was set to be the second in a set of bellwether trials meant to t
item.dc:creator: The Verge

item.title: Google justifies its massive AI spending with a booming cloud business
item.description: Google's cloud business is thriving, as companies adopting its AI and AI infrastructure services help the tech giant to report record profits.
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch

item.title: Medici family mystery may be solved after more than 400 years
item.description: Article URL: https://www.cnn.com/2026/07/15/science/medici-family-mystery-dna-malaria Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=49014007 Points: 81 # Comments: 20
item.dc:creator: Hacker News

item.dc:creator: Hacker News

item.title: Upbound says hack caused $13 million in fraudulent Acima leases
item.description: The Upbound Group fintech company disclosed that threat actors who stole data from its systems leveraged it to create $13 million in Acima leases. [...]
item.dc:creator: BleepingComputer

item.title: Why Lettuce Is Always Making People Sick
item.description: The cyclospora diarrhea outbreak isn’t an isolated incident. It’s part of a pattern of leafy greens wreaking digestive havoc.
item.dc:creator: WIRED

item.title: Malleable Computing, Emacs, and You
item.description: Article URL: http://yummymelon.com/devnull/malleable-computing-emacs-and-you.html Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=49013538 Points: 68 # Comments: 17
item.dc:creator: Hacker News

item.title: Sales were up at Tesla but so were costs and spending
item.description: Q2 2026 was profitable, but barely.
item.dc:creator: Ars Technica

item.title: Safari Technology Preview 248 Released
item.description: Article URL: https://webkit.org/blog/18162/release-notes-for-safari-technology-preview-248/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=49013356 Points: 79 # Comments: 43
item.dc:creator: Hacker News

item.title: The White House Is Trying to Figure Out What to Do About Chinese AI
item.description: There’s a debate going on in the Trump administration over how to handle increasingly powerful Chinese AI models.
item.dc:creator: WIRED

item.title: Treasury threatens sanctions after White House claims Moonshot distilled Anthropic’s Fable
item.description: The episode has also intensified a broader debate in Washington over the influx of Chinese open models.
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch

item.title: Tesla spending skyrockets as Cybercab, Semi, Megapack production timeline slips
item.description: Tesla's 26% boost in revenue wasn't enough to offset rising operating expenses and capital expenditures as it pushes to launch a new generation of products.
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch

item.title: Top Red-Light Therapy Deals at Nordstrom’s Anniversary Sale (2026)
item.description: Save on WIRED-tested LED therapy devices from TheraFace, HigherDose, and more.
item.dc:creator: WIRED

item.title: Social media addiction lawsuit against Meta is dropped
item.description: A closely watched social media addiction lawsuit that had been set to go to trial next week has been dropped after the plaintiff voluntarily dismissed his claims against Meta, leaving none of the major tech companies facing trial in the case.
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch

item.title: Microsoft brings original Xbox backward compatibility to Windows PCs
item.description: First four compatible titles can be run with an 11-year-old graphics card.
item.dc:creator: Ars Technica

item.title: I Inspected My Take-Home Interview Project. It Was a Whole Operation
item.description: Article URL: https://citizendot.github.io/articles/fake-job-interview-git-hook-malware/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=49013036 Points: 278 # Comments: 75
item.dc:creator: Hacker News

item.title: Tesla’s revenues are bouncing back, but profits are still weak
item.description: After a dismal two years of weakening demand, falling sales, and damage to its brand by Elon Musk's political activities, Tesla's road to recovery continues apace. On the heels of an impressive delivery report, the company released its earnings for the second quarter of 2026 - giving us the latest g
item.dc:creator: The Verge

item.title: ISPs' long nightmare of having to list all the fees they charge is finally over
item.description: FCC lets ISPs stop listing all fees after companies complained it was too hard.
item.dc:creator: Ars Technica

item.title: Fairphone 6 wide camera experimental Linux support
item.description: Article URL: https://nondescriptpointer.com/articles/fairphone-6-wide-camera-linux/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=49012777 Points: 73 # Comments: 6
item.dc:creator: Hacker News

item.title: South Korea discloses data breach impacting diplomats worldwide
item.description: South Korea disclosed that hackers breached the National Diplomatic Academy's online education system for ten months and stole personal information belonging to current and former employees of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MFA), including overseas diplomats. [...]
item.dc:creator: BleepingComputer

item.title: SoundCloud acquires decentralized music platform Nina Protocol months after its shutdown
item.description: SoundCloud has acquired decentralized music platform Nina Protocol, months after the startup announced it would shut down. The deal brings Nina’s artists, editorial archive, and music discovery tools to SoundCloud as the company continues expanding its platform for independent musicians.
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch

item.title: ‘The Odyssey’ Is Inspiring Unprecedented Levels of Cope
item.description: Elon Musk and the online right convinced themselves that Christopher Nolan’s “woke” epic would go down in flames. Now they’re crashing out.
item.dc:creator: WIRED

item.title: John C. Dvorak has died
item.description: https://xcancel.com/na_announce/status/2079952538040672302 https://www.twit.community/t/john-c-dvorak-has-passed-away/2... https://www.facebook.com/aric.mackey/posts/john-c-dvorak-194... Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=49012070 Points: 575 # Comments: 182
item.dc:creator: Hacker News

item.title: Clayface trailer leans into the body horror
item.description: "The people I trusted betrayed me. The justice system failed me."
item.dc:creator: Ars Technica

item.title: Price-hiked iPads are a little cheaper right now
item.description: A number of Apple products got more expensive last month, so we’re happy to find deals wherever and whenever we can. If you’re searching for a high-end iPad, one of the more notable deals currently happening is on the 13-inch iPad Pro M5 with 512GB of storage. Normally $1,699, it’s $150 off at Amazo
item.dc:creator: The Verge

item.title: iOS code could reportedly let Apple cut off apps when users miss iPhone payments
item.description: Code found in an iOS 27 beta would allow Apple to put a financed iPhone in "Restricted Mode" if it detects any missed payments, 9to5Mac reports. The finding follows a story from Bloomberg earlier this week claiming Apple is preparing to launch a new "Apple Upgrade" financing program for leasing new
item.dc:creator: The Verge

item.title: How OpenAI’s human mistake led to the AI-powered hack on Hugging Face
item.description: OpenAI made a mistake setting up what it called a “highly isolated” testing environment and sandbox. According to cybersecurity experts, that human mistake is what made the AI-powered attack on Hugging Face possible.
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch

item.title: China’s Open AI Models Are Challenging Silicon Valley’s Playbook
item.description: As access to Anthropic’s and OpenAI’s frontier models becomes more restricted, Chinese labs are pitching their open-source alternatives as stable, accessible, and increasingly capable.
item.dc:creator: WIRED

item.title: Clarity didn't work, trying mysterianism (2012)
item.description: Article URL: https://gwern.net/doc/fiction/science-fiction/2012-10-03-yvain-thewhisperingearring.html Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=49011687 Points: 41 # Comments: 9
item.dc:creator: Hacker News

item.title: Travis Kalanick’s robotics company raises $1.7B, led by a16z
item.description: Uber is also investing in Travis Kalanick's company Atoms, which has made gauzy claims about using industrial AI to modernize the world.
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch

item.title: GitHub Cuts Public Bug Bounty Payouts, Moves Top Rewards to VIP Tier
item.description: Beginning July 27, 2026, GitHub will cut public bug bounty payouts by at least half at every severity level. Critical findings will drop from $20,000-$30,000+ to a fixed $10,000, while its permanent invite-only VIP tier will pay $30,000 or more. Reports filed before that date, including those alread
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Hyundai claims humanoid robot plan is not part of talks with striking workers
item.description: Union previously warned automaker that any robot deployment must be negotiated.
item.dc:creator: Ars Technica

item.title: Science Corporation’s vision-restoring chip wins EU approval
item.description: "The thing that the space needs is a company making $100 million a year of revenue," Science Corp. CEO Max Hodak said.
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch

item.title: Yope raises $12.3M to build a private social network without algorithms or ads
item.description: Yope, a fast-growing social app focused on private groups of friends and family, has raised $12.3 million in seed funding. Instead of chasing creators and algorithmic feeds, the startup is betting that the future of social networking lies in small, private communities powered by messaging, photo sha
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch

item.title: Ubuntu snap-confine Flaw Could Give Local Users Root on Default Desktop Installs
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new local privilege escalation (LPE) vulnerability in snap-confine that an unprivileged user can trigger to obtain root access and gain complete control of a target environment. The high-severity flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-8933 (CVSS score: 7.8),
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Menlo Ventures’ Matt Murphy explains why Anthropic is winning (and it’s not the model)
item.description: Anthropic leaped to a $47 billion revenue run rate by May, compared to $9 billion in 2025. It’s the kind of growth that Menlo Ventures’ Matt Murphy says he’s never seen in 25 years of investing, not in the internet wave, not in mobile, not in the first cloud boom. Menlo led Anthropic’s $500M Series
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch

item.title: Show HN: Cactus Hybrid: We taught Gemma 4 to know when it's wrong
item.description: Hey HN, Henry & Roman here from Cactus. A small, on-device model is fast and private, but sometimes wrong, but frontier models are getting expensive pretty fast. So, we post-trained Gemma 4 E2B post-trained to know when it's wrong. Every response comes with a confidence score between 0 and 1. Develo
item.dc:creator: Hacker News

item.title: Monday.com lays off hundreds to focus on AI
item.description: The company said it is reducing its headcount by 20%, or about 630 staff, to "support a leaner, more focused operating model" as it focuses on its AI Work Platform.
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch

item.title: Dyson V10 Konical Review (2026): Affordable and Solid
item.description: Out of Dyson’s two new affordable stick vacuums, this one is easily my favorite.
item.dc:creator: WIRED

item.title: Everyone Should Know SIMD
item.description: Article URL: https://mitchellh.com/writing/everyone-should-know-simd Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=49010648 Points: 262 # Comments: 76
item.dc:creator: Hacker News

item.title: Are AI Labs Pelicanmaxxing?
item.description: Article URL: https://dylancastillo.co/posts/pelicanmaxxing.html Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=49010129 Points: 403 # Comments: 157

item.title: Google is making it easier to switch from iPhone to Android
item.description: The new feature lets users wirelessly transfer more data types from an iPhone without needing to download a separate app.
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
